The study found that only 25% of patients achieved 25-hydroxyvitamin D levels ≥30 ng/ml, and mean iPTH levels did not significantly decrease after treatment. This indicates that the K/DOQI recommended doses of ergocalciferol were largely inadequate for correcting vitamin D deficiency and hyperparathyroidism in CKD patients. Therefore, the correct answer is no.

\boxed{B}
